items.caption: A slide from a presentation, predominantly in shades of blue and white, with a logo in the top left corner. the slide is titled "inventory management (continued)" and provides a definition of "inventory turnover" as the number of times inventory is sold within a one-year period. below the definition, there are bullet points explaining that inventory turnover refers to the cost of goods sold divided by average inventory at cost, with an average inventory level of $200,000 and an average cost of goods sold of $50,000. the slide also notes that inventory is sold 4 times per year and that it compares with competitors or benchmarked companies. the slide is part of a larger presentation, as indicated by the footer text "© 2008 prentice hall" and "college of engineering."
